course_title: Introduction to Occupational Therapy for Graduates

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an understanding of core occupational therapy concepts, including the construct of occupations, the interrelationship between the person, occupation(s) and environment, and their impact on occupational performance and health.
Identify and describe the occupational therapy process and each of its stages, from the point of receiving a referral to the conclusion of occupational therapy services.
Describe how theory informs occupational therapy practice.
Apply and describe the application of the occupational therapy process in clinical practice.
Describe evidence-based practice and its application in the occupational therapy process.
Demonstrate foundational therapeutic communication skills necessary for professional communication.
Demonstrate foundational skills in gathering information from a simulated patient by applying occupational therapy theory.
Demonstrate professional skills by working collaboratively and responsibly with peers as part of a team and professional communication.
Demonstrate knowledge of methods in assessing components of neurological function (such as sensory evaluation, tone, and coordination).
Apply theoretical knowledge of human anatomy to identify and palpate anatomical structures on the living body and perform range of motion (ROM) measurement and manual muscle testing (MMT).
Demonstrate effective communication skills when handling and assessing individuals.

assessments.task_description:
There will be 4 online quizzes in Weeks 3, 5, 8 and 11. The quiz questions will be focused on assessing content associated with students’ learning in the weeks preceding the quizzes. To support students’ preparation for the quizzes, guiding study questions will be provided a week before each quiz. 

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Criteria, Marking and Submission: Please refer to Learn.UQ to access information on the Task Assignment, Marking Criteria and Submission for this assessment item
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
25/09/2026

The timetable will be announced on Learn.UQ prior to the assessment date.
assessments.task_description:
Students will work in pairs to interview a Simulated patient in Week 9. This assessment item will assess students' foundational information gathering skills and professional communication, which are skills essential to the occupational therapy process. 

When a student is required to complete an oral or practical assessment item worth 20% or more, the performance will be recorded and retained per Appendix 1. (Assessment Procedure (95))

This assessment task is to be completed in person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. 

Students are required to attend the assessment in professional attire, as this forms part of the marking rubric. This may include the OT uniform with black dress pants and black shoes, or other appropriate professional attire.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please refer to Learn.UQ to access information on the Task Assignment and Marking Criteria for this assessment item
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Students will be assessed on their skills in assessing muscle strength (manual muscle testing; MMT) and upper limb range of motion (ROM) through a viva. These are considered core skills used as part of the occupational therapy process (assessment/evaluation). Students will work in pairs, where each student must demonstrate 1 MMT and 1 ROM test on their partner during the viva, which will be recorded.

When a student is required to complete an oral or practical assessment item worth 20% or more, the performance will be recorded and retained per Appendix 1. (Assessment Procedure (95))

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Students are required to attend the assessment in professional attire, as this forms part of the marking rubric. This may include the OT uniform with black dress pants and black shoes, or other appropriate professional attire.

assessments.task_description:
Students will work in pairs on a written case assignment. In the assignment, students will be required to demonstrate and apply knowledge of the occupational therapy process taught throughout the course.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Preparation Activities

Preparation activities that are necessary for each week’s learning activities, such as reading materials, will be provided (Weeks 1 to 13). Students are required to complete preparation activities before the weekly lecture and/or tutorial, depending on the learning requirements on the specified week. 


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07
learning_activities.topic_description: Preparation Activities Preparation activities that are necessary for each week’s learning activities, such as reading materials, will be provided (Weeks 1 to 13). Students are required to complete preparation activities before the weekly lecture and/or tutorial, depending on the learning requirements on the specified week.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Lecture Series

Lectures will be delivered online each week from Weeks 1 to 13. They will be recorded to support student learning.


Sub-activity: There will be an additional online lecture in Week 3 for the Simulated Patient Interview Briefing. During some allotted lectures between Weeks 4 to 12, occupational therapy clinicians with different areas of expertise will deliver 1-hour online seminars. Clinicians will provide applied examples of the occupational therapy process based on their area of practice to facilitate students' learning of how knowledge is applied in clinical practice. Students will have the opportunity to clarify their understanding and gain insights into some of the different areas of practice in occupational therapy.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Tutorial Series

Tutorials will be delivered from Weeks 1 to 13. They provide additional learning materials and opportunities for practical application to support the lectures delivered each week. These are designed to support students to consolidate knowledge and reflect on how this knowledge might be applied in practice. Most tutorial content will be delivered face to face, and a few tutorials are designed as self-directed learning (online) activities. Please note that due to the practical nature, tutorials will not be recorded.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 5 To Week 12(24 Aug - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
ROM/MMT Practicals

In the practical sessions, students will apply their knowledge of the anatomy of the upper limb to assess 1) range of motion (ROM) and 2) muscle strength, through manual muscle testing.

Please note that due to its practical nature, tutorials will not be recorded.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L08, L09, L10, L11
